case_summary: Older galvanised supply pipe failing under the garden, soaking lawn and dropping water pressure inside.

ground_truth.notes: Classic burst-pipe presentation — wet patch where there shouldn't be one, plus property-wide pressure drop. Tests routing to burst_pipe_leak rather than the more generic water_pressure_supply (a real burst is the cause; low pressure is the symptom).

inputs.user_text: There's a wet patch in our lawn near the boundary wall that's been getting bigger over the last week, and the water pressure inside the house has dropped quite a bit. We can hear water running even when nothing's on. The house is from the 1970s and we think the pipes underneath are galvanised steel.
